LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS JAMES TURNER III AND NANCY TURNER'S MOTION TO STRIKE CLAIMS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AGAINST DEFENDANTS IS DENIED. THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS ADEQUATELY PLED AGAINST THE MOVING DEFENDANTS. THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER THE ARGUMENT BASED ON VEHICLE CIDE 17151 BECAUSE IT WAS RAISED FOR THE FIRST TIME IN THE REPLY MEMORANDUM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-02-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JONATHAN M. RUTHLEDGE, TEL. NO. 362-8600 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DAVID J. STREZA OF VOGL MEREDITH BURKE LLP, TEL. NO. 398-0200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JAMES TURNER III and NANCY TURNER's MOTION TO STRIKE CLAIMS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AGAINST DEFENDANTS is Denied. The request for punitive damages is adequately pled against the moving defendants. The court did not consider the argument based on Vehicle Cide 17151 because it was raised for the first time in the reply memorandum. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-02-2012 Continued To Apr-27-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610
